title: InnerMate per-message classification
description: The full understanding record produced for every user message BEFORE any reply is generated. Fields marked runtime:true exist today in RiskClassification (src/lib/companion-risk.ts) and are produced deterministically. Fields marked runtime:false are the training-layer extension defined by docs/training/README.md ('Extending the runtime classifier'). A model-assisted pass may populate training-layer fields but must NEVER lower riskLevel or move responseMode away from 'safety'.

risk_level.description: runtime:true (RiskClassification.riskLevel). 0 = no risk. 1 = dark-tinged distress, no ideation. 2 = passive ideation or unclear safety; warm script + safety check. 3 = active danger; deterministic template only, never model-generated.

emotional_state.description: runtime:partial (RiskClassification.primaryEmotion is coarser). Surface feeling in 2-5 plain words, e.g. 'ashamed and small', 'panicked, racing', 'numb after the fight'.

hidden_need.description: runtime:false (training layer; RiskClassification.primaryNeed is coarser). What the person actually needs underneath, never a restatement of the emotion. E.g. 'permission to grieve without a deadline', 'proof one small action still works'.

impulse_risk.description: runtime:false (derivable from NO_IMPULSE signal hits). Likelihood the user acts on an urge (text/call/check/unblock/confront) within the hour. 'high' = drafted message, thumb on send, 'about to'.

mode.description: The training-mode vocabulary (9 modes). Maps onto the 7 runtime ResponseMode values via wire and runtime mapping below; shame_guilt runs as a mirror flavor and spiritual as a deep_thinking flavor until promoted (see modes.md).

runtime_mode.description: runtime:true (RiskClassification.responseMode). The mode value the deterministic classifier emits today.

wire_mode.description: runtime:true (toWireMode). What the chat client renders: facet line, tinted dot, chips. 'safety' is the only mode that shows chips.

use_memory.description: runtime:false. Whether stored memory may be woven into THIS reply. Hard rules: always false when risk_level >= 2, always false on the first exchange of a session, false when the user is mid-impulse (memory of the ex is fuel).

what.description: Which memory category to draw on, when use=true.

how.description: One line on how to reference it softly. Never verbatim quotes, never dates, never 'you told me on...'.

response_length.description: runtime:false. one_line ~15 words for holding silence or confirming; short = 2-3 sentences ~40 words max 55 (the default); medium = 4-6 sentences, ONLY for safety scripts and explicit named-tradition asks.

next_best_action.description: runtime:false. The single thing the companion/app steers toward after this reply. E.g. 'hold the thread, no new question next turn', 'offer 2-minute grounding if activation stays high', 'suggest the unsent letter in Journal', 'point to Insights for the real history'.

safety.description: runtime:true. Deterministic safety flags from RiskClassification. The model can never set these.

should_show_hotline.description: True only at level 3; numbers come only from crisis-resources.ts config.

quick_replies.description: Safety chips only ('I can stay safe' / 'I may not be safe' / 'Ground me for 2 minutes' / 'Open SOS'). Empty outside safety mode: normal conversation renders NO chips.

safety_follow_up.description: Set when this message answers a safety check: negation-aware, ambiguity treated as not safe.

carry_over.description: No risk language in this turn but recent risk context; keep the safety posture open softly.

third_party.description: The user is describing someone ELSE at risk; route to support-the-supporter.

reason.description: runtime:true. One-line machine explanation of the classification, for audit logs.

mode_mappings.description: Training mode -> (runtime ResponseMode, WireMode). Reference table, mirrors toWireMode() plus the two prompt-level flavors.

hard_invariants.description: Cross-field rules every valid record obeys. The golden-library test enforces these.
const: risk_level == 3 implies mode == emergency and reply is the deterministic template from crisis-resources.ts
risk_level == 2 implies mode == emergency, response_length == medium, safety.should_ask_safety_check == true
safety.quick_replies non-empty only when wire_mode == safety
impulse_risk == high implies mode is no_impulse or emergency
response_length == medium only for emergency or an explicit named-tradition ask
ambiguous answer to a safety check is treated as not_safe, never confirmed_safe
